function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.
Important: cloud sandboxes may be cleaned up, idled out, or recreated between turns. Persistent filesystem means files can resume later; it does NOT guarantee a continuously running machine or surviving background processes. Use terminal sandboxes for task work, not durable hosting.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

check_interval.description: Seconds between automatic status checks for background processes (gateway/messaging only, minimum 30). When set, I'll proactively report progress.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified when the process finishes — no polling needed. Use this for tasks that take a while (tests, builds, deployments) so you can keep working on other things in the meantime.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits.

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

[Subdirectory context discovered: /tmp/mc-docs/AGENTS.md]
# Repository Guidelines

## Project Structure & Module Organization
- `backend/`: FastAPI service. Main app code lives in `backend/app/` with API routes in `backend/app/api/`, data models in `backend/app/models/`, schemas in `backend/app/schemas/`, and service logic in `backend/app/services/`.
- `backend/migrations/`: Alembic migrations (`backend/migrations/versions/` for generated revisions).
- `backend/tests/`: pytest suite (`test_*.py` naming).
- `backend/templates/`: backend-shipped templates used by gateway flows.
- `frontend/`: Next.js app. Routes under `frontend/src/app/`, shared components under `frontend/src/components/`, utilities under `frontend/src/lib/`.
- `frontend/src/api/generated/`: generated API client; regenerate instead of editing by hand.
- `docs/`: contributor and operations docs (start at `docs/README.md`).

## Build, Test, and Development Commands
- `make setup`: install/sync backend and frontend dependencies.
- `make check`: closest CI parity run (lint, typecheck, tests/coverage, frontend build).
- `docker compose -f compose.yml --env-file .env up -d --build`: run full stack.
- Fast local loop:
  - `docker compose -f compose.yml --env-file .env up -d db`
  - `cd backend && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000`
  - `cd frontend && npm run dev`
- `make api-gen`: regenerate frontend API client (backend must be on `127.0.0.1:8000`).

## Coding Style & Naming Conventions
- Python: Black + isort + flake8 + strict mypy. Max line length is 100. Use `snake_case`.
- TypeScript/React: ESLint + Prettier. Components use `PascalCase`; variables/functions use `camelCase`.
- For intentionally unused destructured TS variables, prefix with `_` to satisfy lint config.

## Testing Guidelines
- Backend: pytest via `make backend-test`; coverage policy via `make backend-coverage` (writes `backend/coverage.xml` and `backend/coverage.json`).
- Frontend: vitest + Testing Library via `make frontend-test` (coverage in `frontend/coverage/`).
- Add or update tests whenever behavior changes.

## Commit & Pull Request Guidelines
- Follow Conventional Commits (seen in history), e.g. `feat: ...`, `fix: ...`, `docs: ...`, `test(core): ...`.
- Keep PRs focused and based on latest `master`.
- Include: what changed, why, test evidence (`make check` or targeted commands), linked issue, and screenshots/logs when UI or operator workflow changes.

## Security & Configuration Tips
- Never commit secrets. Copy from `.env.example` and keep real values in local `.env`.
- Report vulnerabilities privately via GitHub security advisories, not public issues.

   202|    "put": "Replace",
   203|    "patch": "Update",
   204|    "delete": "Delete",
   205|}
   206|
   207|
   208|def _resolve_schema_ref(
   209|    schema: dict[str, Any],
   210|    *,
   211|    components: dict[str, Any],
   212|    seen_refs: set[str] | None = None,
   213|) -> dict[str, Any]:
   214|    """Resolve local component refs for OpenAPI schema traversal."""
   215|    ref = schema.get("$ref")
   216|    if not isinstance(ref, str):
   217|        return schema
   218|    if not ref.startswith(_JSON_SCHEMA_REF_PREFIX):
   219|        return schema
   220|    if seen_refs is None:
   221|        seen_refs = set()
   222|    if ref in seen_refs:
   223|        return schema
   224|    seen_refs.add(ref)
   225|    schema_name = ref[len(_JSON_SCHEMA_REF_PREFIX) :]
   226|    schemas = components.get("schemas")
   227|    if not isinstance(schemas, dict):
   228|        return schema
   229|    target = schemas.get(schema_name)
   230|    if not isinstance(target, dict):
   231|        return schema
   232|    return _resolve_schema_ref(target, components=components, seen_refs=seen_refs)
   233|
   234|
   235|def _example_from_schema(schema: dict[str, Any], *, components: dict[str, Any]) -> Any:
   236|    """Generate an OpenAPI example from schema metadata with sensible fallbacks."""
   237|    resolved = _resolve_schema_ref(schema, components=components)
   238|
   239|    if "example" in resolved:
   240|        return resolved["example"]
   241|    examples = resolved.get("examples")
   242|    if isinstance(examples, list) and examples:
   243|        return examples[0]
   244|
   245|    for composite_key in ("anyOf", "oneOf", "allOf"):
   246|        composite = resolved.get(composite_key)
   247|        if isinstance(composite, list):
   248|            for branch in composite:
   249|                if not isinstance(branch, dict):
   250|                    continue
   251|                branch_example = _example_from_schema(branch, components=components)
   252|                if branch_example is not None:
   253|                    return branch_example
   254|
   255|    enum_values = resolved.get("enum")
   256|    if isinstance(enum_values, list) and enum_values:
   257|        return enum_values[0]
   258|
   259|    schema_type = resolved.get("type")
   260|    if schema_type == "object":
   261|        output: dict[str, Any] = {}
   262|        properties = resolved.get("properties")
   263|        if isinstance(properties, dict):
   264|            for key, property_schema in properties.items():
   265|                if not isinstance(property_schema, dict):
   266|                    continue
   267|                property_example = _example_from_schema(property_schema, components=components)
   268|                if property_example is not None:
   269|                    output[key] = property_example
   270|        if output:
   271|            return output
   272|        additional_properties = resolved.get("additionalProperties")
   273|        if isinstance(additional_properties, dict):
   274|            value_example = _example_from_schema(additional_properties, components=components)
   275|            if value_example is not None:
   276|                return {"key": value_example}
   277|        return {}
   278|
   279|    if schema_type == "array":
   280|        items = resolved.get("items")
   281|        if isinstance(items, dict):
   282|            item_example = _example_from_schema(items, components=components)
   283|            if item_example is not None:
   284|                return [item_example]
   285|        return []
   286|
   287|    if schema_type == "string":
   288|        return "string"
   289|    if schema_type == "integer":
   290|        return 0
   291|    if schema_type == "number":
   292|        return 0
   293|    if schema_type == "boolean":
   294|        return False
   295|
   296|    return None
   297|
   298|
   299|def _inject_json_content_example(
   300|    *,
   301|    content: dict[str, Any],
   302|    components: dict[str, Any],
   303|) -> None:
   304|    """Attach an example to application/json content when one is missing."""
   305|    app_json = content.get("application/json")
   306|    if not isinstance(app_json, dict):
   307|        return
   308|    if "example" in app_json or "examples" in app_json:
   309|        return
   310|    schema = app_json.get("schema")
   311|    if not isinstance(schema, dict):
   312|        return
   313|    generated_example = _example_from_schema(schema, components=components)
   314|    if generated_example is not None:
   315|        app_json["example"] = generated_example
   316|
   317|
   318|def _build_operation_summary(*, method: str, path: str) -> str:
   319|    """Build a readable summary when an operation does not define one."""
   320|    prefix = _METHOD_SUMMARY_PREFIX.get(method.lower(), "Handle")
   321|    path_without_prefix = path.removeprefix("/api/v1/")
   322|    parts = [
   323|        part.replace("-", " ")
   324|        for part in path_without_prefix.split("/")
   325|        if part and not (part.startswith("{") and part.endswith("}"))
   326|    ]
   327|    if not parts:
   328|        return prefix
   329|    return f"{prefix} {' '.join(parts)}".strip().title()
   330|
   331|
   332|def _normalize_operation_docs(
   333|    *,
   334|    operation: dict[str, Any],
   335|    method: str,
   336|    path: str,
   337|) -> None:
   338|    """Normalize summary/description/responses/request-body docs for tagged operations."""
   339|    summary = str(operation.get("summary", "")).strip()
   340|    if not summary:
   341|        summary = _build_operation_summary(method=method, path=path)
   342|        operation["summary"] = summary
   343|
   344|    description = str(operation.get("description", "")).strip()
   345|    if not description:
   346|        operation["description"] = f"{summary}."
   347|
   348|    request_body = operation.get("requestBody")
   349|    if isinstance(request_body, dict):
   350|        if not str(request_body.get("description", "")).strip():
   351|            request_body["description"] = "JSON request payload."
   352|
   353|    responses = operation.get("responses")
   354|    if not isinstance(responses, dict):
   355|        return
   356|    for status_code, response in responses.items():
   357|        if not isinstance(response, dict):
   358|            continue
   359|        existing_description = str(response.get("description", "")).strip()
   360|        if not existing_description or existing_description in _GENERIC_RESPONSE_DESCRIPTIONS:
   361|            response["description"] = _HTTP_RESPONSE_DESCRIPTIONS.get(
   362|                str(status_code),
   363|                "Request processed.",
   364|            )
   365|
   366|
   367|def _inject_tagged_operation_openapi_docs(openapi_schema: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   368|    """Ensure targeted-tag operations expose consistent OpenAPI docs and examples."""
   369|    components = openapi_schema.get("components")
   370|    if not isinstance(components, dict):
   371|        return
   372|    paths = openapi_schema.get("paths")
   373|    if not isinstance(paths, dict):
   374|        return
   375|
   376|    for path, path_item in paths.items():
   377|        if not isinstance(path_item, dict):
   378|            continue
   379|        for method, operation in path_item.items():
   380|            if not isinstance(operation, dict):
   381|                continue
   382|            tags = operation.get("tags")
   383|            if not isinstance(tags, list):
   384|                continue
   385|            if not _OPENAPI_EXAMPLE_TAGS.intersection(tags):
   386|                continue
   387|
   388|            _normalize_operation_docs(operation=operation, method=method, path=path)
   389|
   390|            request_body = operation.get("requestBody")
   391|            if isinstance(request_body, dict):
   392|                request_content = request_body.get("content")
   393|                if isinstance(request_content, dict):
   394|                    _inject_json_content_example(content=request_content, components=components)
   395|
   396|            responses = operation.get("responses")
   397|            if isinstance(responses, dict):
   398|                for response in responses.values():
   399|                    if not isinstance(response, dict):
   400|                        continue
   401|                    response_content = response.get("content")
   402|                    if isinstance(response_content, dict):
   403|                        _inject_json_content_example(
   404|                            content=response_content, components=components
   405|                        )
   406|
   407|
   408|def _build_custom_openapi(fastapi_app: FastAPI) -> dict[str, Any]:
   409|    """Generate OpenAPI schema with normalized docs/examples for targeted tags."""
   410|    if fastapi_app.openapi_schema:
   411|        return fastapi_app.openapi_schema
   412|    openapi_schema = get_openapi(
   413|        title=fastapi_app.title,
   414|        version=fastapi_app.version,
   415|        openapi_version=fastapi_app.openapi_version,
   416|        description=fastapi_app.description,
   417|        routes=fastapi_app.routes,
   418|        tags=fastapi_app.openapi_tags,
   419|        servers=fastapi_app.servers,
   420|    )
   421|    _inject_tagged_operation_openapi_docs(openapi_schema)
   422|    fastapi_app.openapi_schema = openapi_schema
   423|    return fastapi_app.openapi_schema
   424|
   425|
   426|class MissionControlFastAPI(FastAPI):
   427|    """FastAPI application with custom OpenAPI normalization."""
   428|
   429|    def openapi(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   430|        return _build_custom_openapi(self)

   447|    logger.info("app.lifecycle.started")
   448|    try:

   174|def _truncate_preview(raw: str, max_chars: int) -> str:
   175|    if len(raw) <= max_chars:
   176|        return raw
   177|    if max_chars <= 3:
   178|        return raw[:max_chars]
   179|    return f"{raw[: max_chars - 3]}..."
   180|
   181|
   182|def _payload_preview_with_limit(
   183|    value: dict[str, object] | list[object] | str | int | float | bool | None,
   184|    *,
   185|    max_chars: int,
   186|) -> tuple[str, bool]:
   187|    if isinstance(value, str):
   188|        return _truncate_preview(value, max_chars), len(value) > max_chars
   189|
   190|    try:
   191|        # Stream JSON chunks so we can stop once we know truncation is required.
   192|        encoder = json.JSONEncoder(ensure_ascii=True)
   193|        parts: list[str] = []
   194|        current_len = 0
   195|        truncated = False
   196|        for chunk in encoder.iterencode(value):
   197|            remaining = (max_chars + 1) - current_len
   198|            if remaining <= 0:
   199|                truncated = True
   200|                break
   201|            if len(chunk) <= remaining:
   202|                parts.append(chunk)
   203|                current_len += len(chunk)
   204|                continue
   205|            parts.append(chunk[:remaining])
   206|            current_len += remaining
   207|            truncated = True
   208|            break
   209|        raw = "".join(parts)
   210|    except TypeError:
   211|        raw = str(value)
   212|        return _truncate_preview(raw, max_chars), len(raw) > max_chars
   213|
   214|    if len(raw) > max_chars:
   215|        truncated = True
   216|    if not truncated:
   217|        return raw, False
   218|    return _truncate_preview(raw, max_chars), True

   360|async def list_boards(
   361|    session: AsyncSession = SESSION_DEP,
   362|    agent_ctx: AgentAuthContext = AGENT_CTX_DEP,
   363|) -> LimitOffsetPage[BoardRead]:
   364|    """List boards visible to the authenticated agent.
   365|
   366|    Board-scoped agents typically see only their assigned board.
   367|    Main agents may see multiple boards when permitted by auth scope.
   368|    """
   369|    statement = select(Board)
   370|    if agent_ctx.agent.board_id:
   371|        statement = statement.where(col(Board.id) == agent_ctx.agent.board_id)
   372|    else:
   373|        # Main agents (board_id=None) must be scoped to their organization
   374|        # via their gateway to prevent cross-tenant board leakage.
   375|        gateway = await Gateway.objects.by_id(agent_ctx.agent.gateway_id).first(session)
   376|        if gateway is None:
   377|            raise HTTPException(
   378|                status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
   379|                detail="Agent gateway not found; cannot determine organization scope.",
   380|            )
   381|        statement = statement.where(
   382|            col(Board.organization_id) == gateway.organization_id,
   383|        )
   384|    statement = statement.order_by(col(Board.created_at).desc())
   385|    return await paginate(session, statement)

    68|def _format_board_field_value(value: object) -> str:
    69|    if isinstance(value, datetime):
    70|        return value.isoformat()
    71|    if isinstance(value, UUID):
    72|        return str(value)
    73|    if isinstance(value, dict):
    74|        return json.dumps(value, sort_keys=True, default=str)
    75|    if isinstance(value, bool):
    76|        return "true" if value else "false"
    77|    if value is None:
    78|        return "null"
    79|    return str(value)
    80|

    82|def _board_update_message(
    83|    *,
    84|    board: Board,
    85|    changed_fields: dict[str, tuple[object, object]],
    86|) -> str:
    87|    lines = [
    88|        "BOARD UPDATED",
    89|        f"Board: {board.name}",
    90|        f"Board ID: {board.id}",
    91|        "",
    92|        "Changed fields:",
    93|    ]
    94|    for field_name in sorted(changed_fields):
    95|        previous, current = changed_fields[field_name]
    96|        lines.append(
    97|            f"- {field_name}: {_format_board_field_value(previous)}"
    98|            f" -> {_format_board_field_value(current)}"
    99|        )
   100|    lines.append("")
   101|    lines.append("Take action: review the board changes and adjust plan/assignments as needed.")
   102|    return "\n".join(lines)

   189|async def _apply_board_update(
   190|    *,
   191|    payload: BoardUpdate,
   192|    session: AsyncSession,
   193|    board: Board,
   194|) -> Board:
   195|    updates = payload.model_dump(exclude_unset=True)
   196|    if "gateway_id" in updates:
   197|        await _require_gateway(
   198|            session,
   199|            updates["gateway_id"],
   200|            organization_id=board.organization_id,
   201|        )
   202|    if "board_group_id" in updates and updates["board_group_id"] is not None:
   203|        await _require_board_group(
   204|            session,
   205|            updates["board_group_id"],
   206|            organization_id=board.organization_id,
   207|        )
   208|    crud.apply_updates(board, updates)
   209|    if updates.get("board_type") == "goal" and (not board.objective or not board.success_metrics):
   210|        # Validate only when explicitly switching to goal boards.
   211|        raise HTTPException(
   212|            status_code=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_CONTENT,
   213|            detail="Goal boards require objective and success_metrics",
   214|        )
   215|    if not board.gateway_id:
   216|        raise HTTPException(
   217|            status_code=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_CONTENT,
   218|            detail="gateway_id is required",
   219|        )
   220|    await _require_gateway(
   221|        session,
   222|        board.gateway_id,
   223|        organization_id=board.organization_id,
   224|    )
   225|    board.updated_at = utcnow()
   226|    return await crud.save(session, board)

   229|def _board_group_change_message(
   230|    *,
   231|    action: Literal["join", "leave"],
   232|    changed_board: Board,
   233|    recipient_board: Board,
   234|    group: BoardGroup,
   235|) -> str:
   236|    changed_label = "Joined Board" if action == "join" else "Left Board"
   237|    guidance = (
   238|        "1) Use cross-board discussion when work spans multiple boards.\n"
   239|        "2) Check related board activity before acting on shared concerns.\n"
   240|        "3) Explicitly coordinate ownership to avoid duplicate or conflicting work.\n"
   241|    )
   242|    if action == "leave":
   243|        guidance = (
   244|            "1) Treat cross-board coordination with the departed board as inactive.\n"
   245|            "2) Re-check dependencies and ownership that previously spanned this board.\n"
   246|            "3) Confirm no in-flight handoffs still rely on the prior group link.\n"
   247|        )
   248|    return (
   249|        "BOARD GROUP UPDATED\n"
   250|        f"{changed_label}: {changed_board.name}\n"
   251|        f"{changed_label} ID: {changed_board.id}\n"
   252|        f"Recipient Board: {recipient_board.name}\n"
   253|        f"Recipient Board ID: {recipient_board.id}\n"
   254|        f"Board Group: {group.name}\n"
   255|        f"Board Group ID: {group.id}\n\n"
   256|        "Coordination guidance:\n"
   257|        f"{guidance}"
   258|    )
   259|
   260|
   261|async def _notify_agents_on_board_group_change(
   262|    *,
   263|    session: AsyncSession,
   264|    board: Board,
   265|    group: BoardGroup,
   266|    action: Literal["join", "leave"],
   267|) -> None:
   268|    dispatch = GatewayDispatchService(session)
   269|    group_boards = await Board.objects.filter_by(board_group_id=group.id).all(session)
   270|    board_by_id = {item.id: item for item in group_boards}
   271|    board_by_id.setdefault(board.id, board)
   272|    board_ids = list(board_by_id.keys())
   273|    if not board_ids:
   274|        return
   275|    agents = await Agent.objects.by_field_in("board_id", board_ids).all(session)
   276|    if not agents:
   277|        return
   278|
   279|    config_by_board_id: dict[UUID, GatewayClientConfig] = {}
   280|    for group_board in board_by_id.values():
   281|        config = await dispatch.optional_gateway_config_for_board(group_board)
   282|        if config is None:
   283|            logger.warning(
   284|                "board.group.%s.notify_skipped board_id=%s group_id=%s target_board_id=%s "
   285|                "reason=no_gateway_config",
   286|                action,
   287|                board.id,
   288|                group.id,
   289|                group_board.id,
   290|            )
   291|            continue
   292|        config_by_board_id[group_board.id] = config
   293|
   294|    if not config_by_board_id:
   295|        logger.warning(
   296|            "board.group.%s.notify_skipped board_id=%s group_id=%s reason=no_gateway_config_any_board",
   297|            action,
   298|            board.id,
   299|            group.id,
   300|        )
   301|        return
   302|
   303|    message_by_board_id = {
   304|        recipient_board_id: _board_group_change_message(
   305|            action=action,
   306|            changed_board=board,
   307|            recipient_board=recipient_board,
   308|            group=group,
   309|        )
   310|        for recipient_board_id, recipient_board in board_by_id.items()
   311|    }
   312|
   313|    notified = 0
   314|    failed = 0
   315|    skipped_missing_session = 0
   316|    skipped_missing_config = 0
   317|    skipped_missing_board = 0
   318|    for agent in agents:
   319|        if not agent.openclaw_session_id:
   320|            skipped_missing_session += 1
   321|            continue
   322|        if agent.board_id is None:
   323|            skipped_missing_board += 1
   324|            continue
   325|        config = config_by_board_id.get(agent.board_id)
   326|        message = message_by_board_id.get(agent.board_id)
   327|        recipient_board = board_by_id.get(agent.board_id)
   328|        if config is None or message is None or recipient_board is None:
   329|            skipped_missing_config += 1
   330|            continue
   331|        error = await dispatch.try_send_agent_message(
   332|            session_key=agent.openclaw_session_id,
   333|            config=config,
   334|            agent_name=agent.name,
   335|            message=message,
   336|            deliver=False,
   337|        )
   338|        if error is None:
   339|            notified += 1
   340|            record_activity(
   341|                session,
   342|                event_type=f"board.group.{action}.notified",
   343|                message=(
   344|                    f"Board-group {action} notice sent to {agent.name} for board "
   345|                    f"{recipient_board.name} related to {board.name} and {group.name}."
   346|                ),
   347|                agent_id=agent.id,
   348|                board_id=recipient_board.id,
   349|            )
   350|        else:
   351|            failed += 1
   352|            record_activity(
   353|                session,
   354|                event_type=f"board.group.{action}.notify_failed",
   355|                message=(
   356|                    f"Board-group {action} notify failed for {agent.name} on board "
   357|                    f"{recipient_board.name}: {error}"
   358|                ),
   359|                agent_id=agent.id,
   360|                board_id=recipient_board.id,
   361|            )
   362|
   363|    if notified or failed:
   364|        await session.commit()
   365|    logger.info(
   366|        "board.group.%s.notify_complete board_id=%s group_id=%s boards_total=%s agents_total=%s "
   367|        "agents_notified=%s agents_failed=%s agents_skipped_no_session=%s "
   368|        "agents_skipped_no_gateway=%s agents_skipped_no_board=%s",
   369|        action,
   370|        board.id,
   371|        group.id,
   372|        len(board_by_id),
   373|        len(agents),
   374|        notified,
   375|        failed,
   376|        skipped_missing_session,
   377|        skipped_missing_config,
   378|        skipped_missing_board,
   379|    )

   410|async def _notify_lead_on_board_update(
   411|    *,
   412|    session: AsyncSession,
   413|    board: Board,
   414|    changed_fields: dict[str, tuple[object, object]],
   415|) -> None:
   416|    if not changed_fields:
   417|        return
   418|    lead = (
   419|        await Agent.objects.filter_by(board_id=board.id)
   420|        .filter(col(Agent.is_board_lead).is_(True))
   421|        .first(session)
   422|    )
   423|    if lead is None or not lead.openclaw_session_id:

   425|    dispatch = GatewayDispatchService(session)

   427|    if config is None:

   429|    message = _board_update_message(

   433|    error = await dispatch.try_send_agent_message(
   434|        session_key=lead.openclaw_session_id,
   435|        config=config,
   436|        agent_name=lead.name,
   437|        message=message,
   438|        deliver=False,
   439|    )
   440|    if error is None:
   441|        record_activity(
   442|            session,
   443|            event_type="board.lead_notified",
   444|            message=f"Lead agent notified for board update: {board.name}.",
   445|            agent_id=lead.id,
   446|            board_id=board.id,
   447|        )
   448|    else:
   449|        record_activity(
   450|            session,
   451|            event_type="board.lead_notify_failed",
   452|            message=f"Lead board update notify failed for {board.name}: {error}",
   453|            agent_id=lead.id,
   454|            board_id=board.id,
   455|        )
   456|    await session.commit()

    58|def _slugify(value: str) -> str:
    59|    slug = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", value.lower()).strip("-")
    60|    return slug or uuid4().hex

    63|async def _require_group_access(
    64|    session: AsyncSession,
    65|    *,
    66|    group_id: UUID,
    67|    member: OrganizationMember,
    68|    write: bool,
    69|) -> BoardGroup:
    70|    group = await BoardGroup.objects.by_id(group_id).first(session)
    71|    if group is None:
    72|        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND)
    73|    if group.organization_id != member.organization_id:
    74|        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN)
    75|
    76|    if write and member_all_boards_write(member):
    77|        return group
    78|    if not write and member_all_boards_read(member):
    79|        return group
    80|
    81|    board_ids = [
    82|        board.id for board in await Board.objects.filter_by(board_group_id=group_id).all(session)
    83|    ]
    84|    if not board_ids:
    85|        if is_org_admin(member):
    86|            return group
    87|        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN)
    88|
    89|    allowed_ids = await list_accessible_board_ids(session, member=member, write=write)
    90|    if not set(board_ids).intersection(set(allowed_ids)):
    91|        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN)
    92|    return group

    95|@router.get("", response_model=DefaultLimitOffsetPage[BoardGroupRead])
    96|async def list_board_groups(
    97|    session: AsyncSession = SESSION_DEP,
    98|    ctx: OrganizationContext = ORG_MEMBER_DEP,
    99|) -> LimitOffsetPage[BoardGroupRead]:
   100|    """List board groups in the active organization."""
   101|    if member_all_boards_read(ctx.member):
   102|        statement = select(BoardGroup).where(
   103|            col(BoardGroup.organization_id) == ctx.organization.id,
   104|        )
   105|    else:
   106|        accessible_boards = select(Board.board_group_id).where(
   107|            board_access_filter(ctx.member, write=False),
   108|        )
   109|        statement = select(BoardGroup).where(
   110|            col(BoardGroup.organization_id) == ctx.organization.id,
   111|            col(BoardGroup.id).in_(accessible_boards),
   112|        )
   113|    statement = statement.order_by(func.lower(col(BoardGroup.name)).asc())
   114|    return await paginate(session, statement)

   196|        await _require_group_access(

   211|async def _agents_for_group_heartbeat(
   212|    session: AsyncSession,
   213|    *,
   214|    group_id: UUID,
   215|    include_board_leads: bool,
   216|) -> tuple[dict[UUID, Board], list[Agent]]:
   217|    boards = await Board.objects.filter_by(board_group_id=group_id).all(session)
   218|    board_by_id = {board.id: board for board in boards}
   219|    board_ids = list(board_by_id.keys())
   220|    if not board_ids:

   222|    agents = await Agent.objects.by_field_in("board_id", board_ids).all(session)
   223|    if not include_board_leads:

   225|    return board_by_id, agents

   233|    raw = agent.heartbeat_config
   234|    heartbeat: dict[str, Any] = DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_CONFIG.copy()
   235|    if isinstance(raw, dict):

   237|    heartbeat["every"] = payload.every

   243|async def _sync_gateway_heartbeats(
   244|    session: AsyncSession,
   245|    *,
   246|    board_by_id: dict[UUID, Board],
   247|    agents: list[Agent],
   248|) -> list[UUID]:
   249|    agents_by_gateway_id: dict[UUID, list[Agent]] = {}
   250|    for agent in agents:
   251|        board_id = agent.board_id
   252|        if board_id is None:

   254|        board = board_by_id.get(board_id)
   255|        if board is None or board.gateway_id is None:
   256|            continue
   257|        agents_by_gateway_id.setdefault(board.gateway_id, []).append(agent)
   258|
   259|    failed_agent_ids: list[UUID] = []
   260|    gateway_ids = list(agents_by_gateway_id.keys())
   261|    gateways = await Gateway.objects.by_ids(gateway_ids).all(session)
   262|    gateway_by_id = {gateway.id: gateway for gateway in gateways}
   263|    for gateway_id, gateway_agents in agents_by_gateway_id.items():
   264|        gateway = gateway_by_id.get(gateway_id)
   265|        if gateway is None or not gateway.url or not gateway.workspace_root:
   266|            failed_agent_ids.extend([agent.id for agent in gateway_agents])
   267|            continue
   268|        try:
   269|            await OpenClawGatewayProvisioner().sync_gateway_agent_heartbeats(
   270|                gateway,
   271|                gateway_agents,
   272|            )
   273|        except OpenClawGatewayError:
   274|            failed_agent_ids.extend([agent.id for agent in gateway_agents])
   275|    return failed_agent_ids

   182|async def _task_has_approved_linked_approval(
   183|    session: AsyncSession,
   184|    *,
   185|    board_id: UUID,
   186|    task_id: UUID,
   187|) -> bool:
   188|    linked_approval_ids = select(col(ApprovalTaskLink.approval_id)).where(
   189|        col(ApprovalTaskLink.task_id) == task_id,
   190|    )
   191|    statement = (
   192|        select(col(Approval.id))
   193|        .where(col(Approval.board_id) == board_id)
   194|        .where(col(Approval.status) == "approved")
   195|        .where(
   196|            or_(
   197|                col(Approval.task_id) == task_id,
   198|                col(Approval.id).in_(linked_approval_ids),
   199|            ),
   200|        )
   201|        .limit(1)
   202|    )
   203|    return (await session.exec(statement)).first() is not None

   311|async def has_valid_recent_comment(
   312|    session: AsyncSession,
   313|    task: Task,
   314|    agent_id: UUID | None,
   315|    since: datetime | None,
   316|) -> bool:
   317|    """Check whether the task has a recent non-empty comment by the agent."""
   318|    if agent_id is None or since is None:
   319|        return False
   320|    statement = (
   321|        select(ActivityEvent)
   322|        .where(col(ActivityEvent.task_id) == task.id)
   323|        .where(col(ActivityEvent.event_type) == "task.comment")
   324|        .where(col(ActivityEvent.agent_id) == agent_id)
   325|        .where(col(ActivityEvent.created_at) >= since)
   326|        .order_by(desc(col(ActivityEvent.created_at)))
   327|    )
   328|    event = (await session.exec(statement)).first()
   329|    if event is None or event.message is None:
   330|        return False
   331|    return bool(event.message.strip())

   334|def _parse_since(value: str | None) -> datetime | None:
   335|    """Parse an optional ISO-8601 timestamp into a naive UTC `datetime`.
   336|
   337|    The API accepts either naive timestamps (treated as UTC) or timezone-aware values.
   338|    Returning naive UTC simplifies SQLModel comparisons against stored naive UTC values.
   339|    """
   340|
   341|    if not value:
   342|        return None
   343|
   344|    normalized = value.strip()
   345|    if not normalized:
   346|        return None
   347|
   348|    # Allow common ISO-8601 `Z` suffix (UTC) even though `datetime.fromisoformat` expects `+00:00`.
   349|    normalized = normalized.replace("Z", "+00:00")
   350|
   351|    try:
   352|        parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(normalized)
   353|    except ValueError:
   354|        return None
   355|

   363|def _coerce_task_items(items: Sequence[object]) -> list[Task]:
   364|    """Validate/convert paginated query results to a concrete `list[Task]`.
   365|
   366|    SQLModel pagination helpers return `Sequence[object]`; we validate types early so the
   367|    rest of the route logic can assume real `Task` instances.
   368|    """
   369|
   370|    tasks: list[Task] = []
   371|    for item in items:
   372|        if not isinstance(item, Task):
   373|            msg = "Expected Task items from paginated query"
   374|            raise TypeError(msg)
   375|        tasks.append(item)
   376|    return tasks

   378|
   379|def _coerce_task_event_rows(
   380|    items: Sequence[object],
   381|) -> list[tuple[ActivityEvent, Task | None]]:
   382|    """Normalize DB rows into `(ActivityEvent, Task | None)` tuples.
   383|
   384|    Depending on the SQLAlchemy/SQLModel execution path, result rows may arrive as:
   385|    - real Python tuples, or
   386|    - row-like objects supporting `__len__` and `__getitem__`.
   387|
   388|    This helper centralizes validation so SSE/event-stream logic can assume a stable shape.
   389|    """
   390|
   391|    rows: list[tuple[ActivityEvent, Task | None]] = []
   392|    for item in items:
   393|        first: object
   394|        second: object
   395|
   396|        if isinstance(item, tuple):
   397|            if len(item) != TASK_EVENT_ROW_LEN:
   398|                msg = "Expected (ActivityEvent, Task | None) rows"
   399|                raise TypeError(msg)
   400|            first, second = item
   401|        else:
   402|            try:
   403|                row_len = len(item)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
   404|                first = item[0]  # type: ignore[index]
   405|                second = item[1]  # type: ignore[index]
   406|            except (IndexError, KeyError, TypeError):
   407|                msg = "Expected (ActivityEvent, Task | None) rows"
   408|                raise TypeError(msg) from None
   409|            if row_len != TASK_EVENT_ROW_LEN:
   410|                msg = "Expected (ActivityEvent, Task | None) rows"
   411|                raise TypeError(msg)
   412|
   413|        if isinstance(first, ActivityEvent) and (isinstance(second, Task) or second is None):
   414|            rows.append((first, second))
   415|            continue
   416|
   417|        msg = "Expected (ActivityEvent, Task | None) rows"
   418|        raise TypeError(msg)
   419|    return rows

   444|            return True

   507|async def list_agents(
   508|    board_id: UUID | None = BOARD_ID_QUERY,
   509|    session: AsyncSession = SESSION_DEP,
   510|    agent_ctx: AgentAuthContext = AGENT_CTX_DEP,
   511|) -> LimitOffsetPage[AgentRead]:
   512|    """List agents visible to the caller, optionally filtered by board.
   513|
   514|    Useful for lead delegation and workload balancing.
   515|    """
   516|    statement = select(Agent)
   517|    if agent_ctx.agent.board_id:
   518|        if board_id:
   519|            OpenClawAuthorizationPolicy.require_board_write_access(
   520|                allowed=board_id == agent_ctx.agent.board_id,
   521|            )
   522|        statement = statement.where(Agent.board_id == agent_ctx.agent.board_id)
   523|    elif board_id:
   524|        statement = statement.where(Agent.board_id == board_id)
   525|    statement = statement.order_by(col(Agent.created_at).desc())
   526|
   527|    def _transform(items: Sequence[Any]) -> Sequence[Any]:
   528|        agents = _coerce_agent_items(items)
   529|        return [
   530|            AgentLifecycleService.to_agent_read(
   531|                AgentLifecycleService.with_computed_status(agent),
   532|            )
   533|            for agent in agents
   534|        ]
   535|
   536|    return await paginate(session, statement, transformer=_transform)

   695|

   699|        if was_truncated:

   701|

   537|async def _fetch_task_events(
   538|    session: AsyncSession,
   539|    board_id: UUID,
   540|    since: datetime,
   541|) -> list[tuple[ActivityEvent, Task | None]]:
   542|    task_ids = list(
   543|        await session.exec(select(Task.id).where(col(Task.board_id) == board_id)),
   544|    )
   545|    if not task_ids:
   546|        return []
   547|    statement = (
   548|        select(ActivityEvent, Task)
   549|        .outerjoin(Task, col(ActivityEvent.task_id) == col(Task.id))
   550|        .where(col(ActivityEvent.task_id).in_(task_ids))
   551|        .where(col(ActivityEvent.event_type).in_(TASK_EVENT_TYPES))
   552|        .where(col(ActivityEvent.created_at) >= since)
   553|        .order_by(asc(col(ActivityEvent.created_at)))
   554|    )
   555|    result = await session.execute(statement)
   556|    return _coerce_task_event_rows(list(result.tuples().all()))

   648|async def _latest_task_comment_by_agent(
   649|    session: AsyncSession,
   650|    *,
   651|    task_id: UUID,
   652|    agent_id: UUID,
   653|) -> str | None:
   654|    statement = (
   655|        select(col(ActivityEvent.message))
   656|        .where(col(ActivityEvent.task_id) == task_id)
   657|        .where(col(ActivityEvent.event_type) == "task.comment")
   658|        .where(col(ActivityEvent.agent_id) == agent_id)
   659|        .order_by(desc(col(ActivityEvent.created_at)))
   660|        .limit(1)
   661|    )
   662|    return (await session.exec(statement)).first()

   678|        record_activity(

   682|            agent_id=agent.id,

   686|    except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - best effort wake path

   937|def _status_values(status_filter: str | None) -> list[str]:
   938|    if not status_filter:
   939|        return []
   940|    values = [s.strip() for s in status_filter.split(",") if s.strip()]
   941|    if any(value not in ALLOWED_STATUSES for value in values):
   942|        raise HTTPException(
   943|            status_code=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_CONTENT,
   944|            detail="Unsupported task status filter.",
   945|        )
   946|    return values

   949|async def _organization_custom_field_definitions_for_board(
   950|    session: AsyncSession,
   951|    *,
   952|    board_id: UUID,
   953|) -> dict[str, _BoardCustomFieldDefinition]:
   954|    organization_id = (
   955|        await session.exec(
   956|            select(Board.organization_id).where(col(Board.id) == board_id),
   957|        )
   958|    ).first()
   959|    if organization_id is None:
   960|        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND)
   961|    definitions = list(
   962|        await session.exec(
   963|            select(TaskCustomFieldDefinition)
   964|            .join(
   965|                BoardTaskCustomField,
   966|                col(BoardTaskCustomField.task_custom_field_definition_id)
   967|                == col(TaskCustomFieldDefinition.id),
   968|            )
   969|            .where(
   970|                col(BoardTaskCustomField.board_id) == board_id,
   971|                col(TaskCustomFieldDefinition.organization_id) == organization_id,
   972|            ),
   973|        ),
   974|    )
   975|    return {
   976|        definition.field_key: _BoardCustomFieldDefinition(
   977|            id=definition.id,
   978|            field_key=definition.field_key,
   979|            field_type=cast(TaskCustomFieldType, definition.field_type),
   980|            validation_regex=definition.validation_regex,
   981|            required=definition.required,
   982|            default_value=definition.default_value,
   983|        )
   984|        for definition in definitions
   985|    }

   988|def _reject_unknown_custom_field_keys(
   989|    *,
   990|    custom_field_values: TaskCustomFieldValues,
   991|    definitions_by_key: dict[str, _BoardCustomFieldDefinition],
   992|) -> None:
   993|    unknown_field_keys = sorted(set(custom_field_values) - set(definitions_by_key))
   994|    if not unknown_field_keys:

    52|logger = get_logger(__name__)

    55|STATUS_FILTER_QUERY = Query(default=None, alias="status")
    56|SINCE_QUERY = Query(default=None)
    57|BOARD_READ_DEP = Depends(get_board_for_actor_read)
    58|BOARD_WRITE_DEP = Depends(get_board_for_actor_write)
    59|BOARD_USER_WRITE_DEP = Depends(get_board_for_user_write)
    60|SESSION_DEP = Depends(get_session)

    64|def _parse_since(value: str | None) -> datetime | None:
    65|    if not value:
    66|        return None
    67|    normalized = value.strip()
    68|    if not normalized:
    69|        return None
    70|    normalized = normalized.replace("Z", "+00:00")
    71|    try:
    72|        parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(normalized)
    73|    except ValueError:
    74|        return None
    75|    if parsed.tzinfo is not None:
    76|        return parsed.astimezone(UTC).replace(tzinfo=None)
    77|    return parsed
    78|
    79|
    80|def _approval_updated_at(approval: Approval) -> datetime:
    81|    return approval.resolved_at or approval.created_at
    82|

    84|async def _approval_task_ids_map(
    85|    session: AsyncSession,
    86|    approvals: Sequence[Approval],
    87|) -> dict[UUID, list[UUID]]:
    88|    approval_ids = [approval.id for approval in approvals]
    89|    mapping = await load_task_ids_by_approval(session, approval_ids=approval_ids)
    90|    for approval in approvals:
    91|        if mapping.get(approval.id):
    92|            continue
    93|        if approval.task_id is not None:
    94|            mapping[approval.id] = [approval.task_id]
    95|        else:
    96|            mapping[approval.id] = []
    97|    return mapping

   100|async def _task_titles_by_id(
   101|    session: AsyncSession,
   102|    *,
   103|    task_ids: set[UUID],
   104|) -> dict[UUID, str]:
   105|    if not task_ids:
   106|        return {}
   107|    rows = list(
   108|        await session.exec(
   109|            select(col(Task.id), col(Task.title)).where(col(Task.id).in_(task_ids)),
   110|        ),
   111|    )
   112|    return {task_id: title for task_id, title in rows}
   113|
   114|
   115|def _approval_to_read(
   116|    approval: Approval,
   117|    *,
   118|    task_ids: list[UUID],
   119|    task_titles: list[str],
   120|) -> ApprovalRead:
   121|    primary_task_id = task_ids[0] if task_ids else None
   122|    model = ApprovalRead.model_validate(approval, from_attributes=True)
   123|    return model.model_copy(
   124|        update={
   125|            "task_id": primary_task_id,
   126|            "task_ids": task_ids,
   127|            "task_titles": task_titles,
   128|        },
   129|    )

   132|async def _approval_reads(
   133|    session: AsyncSession,
   134|    approvals: Sequence[Approval],
   135|) -> list[ApprovalRead]:
   136|    mapping = await _approval_task_ids_map(session, approvals)
   137|    title_by_id = await _task_titles_by_id(
   138|        session,
   139|        task_ids={task_id for task_ids in mapping.values() for task_id in task_ids},
   140|    )
   141|    return [
   142|        _approval_to_read(
   143|            approval,
   144|            task_ids=(task_ids := mapping.get(approval.id, [])),
   145|            task_titles=[title_by_id[task_id] for task_id in task_ids if task_id in title_by_id],
   146|        )
   147|        for approval in approvals
   148|    ]

   151|def _serialize_approval(approval: ApprovalRead) -> dict[str, object]:
   152|    return approval.model_dump(mode="json")

   155|def _pending_conflict_detail(conflicts: dict[UUID, UUID]) -> dict[str, object]:
   156|    ordered = sorted(conflicts.items(), key=lambda item: str(item[0]))
   157|    return {
   158|        "message": "Each task can have only one pending approval.",
   159|        "conflicts": [
   160|            {
   161|                "task_id": str(task_id),
   162|                "approval_id": str(approval_id),
   163|            }
   164|            for task_id, approval_id in ordered
   165|        ],
   166|    }
   167|
   168|
   169|async def _ensure_no_pending_approval_conflicts(
   170|    session: AsyncSession,
   171|    *,
   172|    board_id: UUID,
   173|    task_ids: Sequence[UUID],
   174|    exclude_approval_id: UUID | None = None,
   175|) -> None:
   176|    normalized_task_ids = list({*task_ids})
   177|    if not normalized_task_ids:
   178|        return
   179|    await lock_tasks_for_approval(session, task_ids=normalized_task_ids)
   180|    conflicts = await pending_approval_conflicts_by_task(
   181|        session,
   182|        board_id=board_id,
   183|        task_ids=normalized_task_ids,
   184|        exclude_approval_id=exclude_approval_id,
   185|    )
   186|    if conflicts:
   187|        raise HTTPException(
   188|            status_code=status.HTTP_409_CONFLICT,
   189|            detail=_pending_conflict_detail(conflicts),
   190|        )
   191|
   192|
   193|def _approval_resolution_message(
   194|    *,
   195|    board: Board,
   196|    approval: Approval,
   197|    task_ids: Sequence[UUID] | None = None,
   198|) -> str:
   199|    status_text = "approved" if approval.status == "approved" else "rejected"
   200|    lines = [
   201|        "APPROVAL RESOLVED",
   202|        f"Board: {board.name}",
   203|        f"Approval ID: {approval.id}",
   204|        f"Action: {approval.action_type}",
   205|        f"Decision: {status_text}",
   206|        f"Confidence: {approval.confidence}",
   207|    ]
   208|    normalized_task_ids = list(task_ids or [])
   209|    if not normalized_task_ids and approval.task_id is not None:
   210|        normalized_task_ids = [approval.task_id]
   211|    if len(normalized_task_ids) == 1:
   212|        lines.append(f"Task ID: {normalized_task_ids[0]}")
   213|    elif normalized_task_ids:
   214|        lines.append(f"Task IDs: {', '.join(str(value) for value in normalized_task_ids)}")
   215|    lines.append("")
   216|    lines.append("Take action: continue execution using the final approval decision.")
   217|    return "\n".join(lines)

   220|async def _resolve_board_lead(
   221|    session: AsyncSession,
   222|    *,
   223|    board_id: UUID,
   224|) -> Agent | None:
   225|    return (
   226|        await Agent.objects.filter_by(board_id=board_id)
   227|        .filter(col(Agent.is_board_lead).is_(True))
   228|        .first(session)
   229|    )

   231|
   232|async def _notify_lead_on_approval_resolution(
   233|    *,
   234|    session: AsyncSession,
   235|    board: Board,
   236|    approval: Approval,
   237|) -> None:
   238|    if approval.status not in {"approved", "rejected"}:
   239|        return
   240|    lead = await _resolve_board_lead(session, board_id=board.id)
   241|    if lead is None or not lead.openclaw_session_id:
   242|        return
   243|
   244|    dispatch = GatewayDispatchService(session)
   245|    config = await dispatch.optional_gateway_config_for_board(board)
   246|    if config is None:
   247|        return
   248|
   249|    task_ids_by_approval = await load_task_ids_by_approval(session, approval_ids=[approval.id])
   250|    message = _approval_resolution_message(
   251|        board=board,
   252|        approval=approval,
   253|        task_ids=task_ids_by_approval.get(approval.id, []),
   254|    )
   255|    error = await dispatch.try_send_agent_message(
   256|        session_key=lead.openclaw_session_id,
   257|        config=config,
   258|        agent_name=lead.name,
   259|        message=message,
   260|        deliver=False,
   261|    )
   262|    if error is None:
   263|        record_activity(
   264|            session,
   265|            event_type="approval.lead_notified",
   266|            message=f"Lead agent notified for {approval.status} approval {approval.id}.",
   267|            agent_id=lead.id,
   268|            task_id=approval.task_id,
   269|            board_id=approval.board_id,
   270|        )
   271|    else:
   272|        record_activity(
   273|            session,
   274|            event_type="approval.lead_notify_failed",
   275|            message=f"Lead notify failed for approval {approval.id}: {error}",
   276|            agent_id=lead.id,
   277|            task_id=approval.task_id,
   278|            board_id=approval.board_id,
   279|        )
   280|    await session.commit()

   283|async def _fetch_approval_events(
   284|    session: AsyncSession,
   285|    board_id: UUID,
   286|    since: datetime,
   287|) -> list[Approval]:
   288|    statement = (
   289|        Approval.objects.filter_by(board_id=board_id)
   290|        .filter(
   291|            or_(
   292|                col(Approval.created_at) >= since,
   293|                col(Approval.resolved_at) >= since,
   294|            ),
   295|        )
   296|        .order_by(asc(col(Approval.created_at)))
   297|    )
   298|    return await statement.all(session)

   301|@router.get("", response_model=DefaultLimitOffsetPage[ApprovalRead])
   302|async def list_approvals(
   303|    status_filter: ApprovalStatus | None = STATUS_FILTER_QUERY,
   304|    board: Board = BOARD_READ_DEP,
   305|    session: AsyncSession = SESSION_DEP,
   306|    _actor: ActorContext = ACTOR_DEP,
   307|) -> LimitOffsetPage[ApprovalRead]:
   308|    """List approvals for a board, optionally filtering by status."""
   309|    statement = Approval.objects.filter_by(board_id=board.id)
   310|    if status_filter:
   311|        statement = statement.filter(col(Approval.status) == status_filter)
   312|    statement = statement.order_by(col(Approval.created_at).desc())
   313|
   314|    async def _transform(items: Sequence[object]) -> Sequence[ApprovalRead]:
   315|        approvals: list[Approval] = []
   316|        for item in items:
   317|            if not isinstance(item, Approval):
   318|                msg = "Expected Approval items from approvals pagination query."
   319|                raise TypeError(msg)
   320|            approvals.append(item)
   321|        return await _approval_reads(session, approvals)
   322|
   323|    return await paginate(session, statement.statement, transformer=_transform)

   326|@router.get("/stream")
   327|async def stream_approvals(
   328|    request: Request,
   329|    board: Board = BOARD_READ_DEP,
   330|    _actor: ActorContext = ACTOR_DEP,
   331|    since: str | None = SINCE_QUERY,
   332|) -> EventSourceResponse:
   333|    """Stream approval updates for a board using server-sent events."""
   334|    since_dt = _parse_since(since) or utcnow()
   335|    last_seen = since_dt
   336|
   337|    async def event_generator() -> AsyncIterator[dict[str, str]]:
   338|        nonlocal last_seen
   339|        while True:
   340|            if await request.is_disconnected():
   341|                break
   342|            async with async_session_maker() as session:
   343|                approvals = await _fetch_approval_events(session, board.id, last_seen)
   344|                approval_reads = await _approval_reads(session, approvals)
   345|                pending_approvals_count = int(
   346|                    (
   347|                        await session.exec(
   348|                            select(func.count(col(Approval.id)))
   349|                            .where(col(Approval.board_id) == board.id)
   350|                            .where(col(Approval.status) == "pending"),
   351|                        )
   352|                    ).one(),
   353|                )
   354|                task_ids = {
   355|                    task_id
   356|                    for approval_read in approval_reads
   357|                    for task_id in approval_read.task_ids
   358|                }
   359|                counts_by_task_id = await task_counts_for_board(
   360|                    session,
   361|                    board_id=board.id,
   362|                    task_ids=task_ids,
   363|                )
   364|            for approval, approval_read in zip(approvals, approval_reads, strict=True):
   365|                updated_at = _approval_updated_at(approval)
   366|                last_seen = max(updated_at, last_seen)
   367|                payload: dict[str, object] = {
   368|                    "approval": _serialize_approval(approval_read),
   369|                    "pending_approvals_count": pending_approvals_count,
   370|                }
   371|                task_counts = [
   372|                    {
   373|                        "task_id": str(task_id),
   374|                        "approvals_count": total,
   375|                        "approvals_pending_count": pending,
   376|                    }
   377|                    for task_id in approval_read.task_ids
   378|                    if (counts := counts_by_task_id.get(task_id)) is not None
   379|                    for total, pending in [counts]
   380|                ]
   381|                if len(task_counts) == 1:
   382|                    payload["task_counts"] = task_counts[0]
   383|                elif task_counts:
   384|                    payload["task_counts"] = task_counts
   385|                yield {"event": "approval", "data": json.dumps(payload)}
   386|            await asyncio.sleep(STREAM_POLL_SECONDS)
   387|
   388|    return EventSourceResponse(event_generator(), ping=15)
   389|

   423|    await replace_approval_task_links(

   428|    await session.commit()
   429|    await session.refresh(approval)
   430|    title_by_id = await _task_titles_by_id(session, task_ids=set(task_ids))
   431|    return _approval_to_read(
   432|        approval,

   438|@router.patch("/{approval_id}", response_model=ApprovalRead)
   439|async def update_approval(
   440|    approval_id: str,
   441|    payload: ApprovalUpdate,
   442|    board: Board = BOARD_USER_WRITE_DEP,
   443|    session: AsyncSession = SESSION_DEP,
   444|) -> ApprovalRead:
   445|    """Update an approval's status and resolution timestamp."""
   446|    approval = await Approval.objects.by_id(approval_id).first(session)
   447|    if approval is None or approval.board_id != board.id:
   448|        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND)
   449|    updates = payload.model_dump(exclude_unset=True)
   450|    prior_status = approval.status
   451|    if "status" in updates:

   453|        if target_status == "pending" and prior_status != "pending":

   460|            await _ensure_no_pending_approval_conflicts(

   467|        if approval.status != "pending":

   479|        except Exception:

   484|                approval.status,
   485|            )
   486|    reads = await _approval_reads(session, [approval])
